title: Using qPCR to Combat Mislabelling of Animal-Based Food Products
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Mislabelling     of     animal-based     food     products     is     a     growing   global   concern  ,   threatening     consumer     trust  ,   food     safety  ,   and     fair     trade  .   Conventional     methods     of     species     identification   are   often     slow     and     unreliable     when     dealing     with     processed     products  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   provides     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     tool     for     authenticating     meat  ,   dairy  ,   and     seafood     products  .   Its     ability     to     detect     trace     amounts     of   DNA   makes     it     highly     effective     against     food     fraud  ,   especially   in   complex     food     matrices  .   Integrating     qPCR     into     national     food     safety     programs     can     strengthen     regulatory     enforcement  ,   protect     religious     dietary     requirements   (e.g., Halal,   Kosher  ),   and     ensure     compliance     with     international     trade     standards  .   Adoption     of     qPCR-based     authentication     contributes     to     improved     transparency  ,   consumer     protection  ,   and     overall     food     system     integrity  .
